alerts.title: Storm / strong wind
alerts.message: Storm risk is 58.8% (medium) because maximum forecast gusts are 22.6 m/s and CAPE is 0 J/kg. The high-risk wind reference is 25.0 m/s; CAPE above 2500 J/kg indicates strong convective instability.

predictions.explanation: Zone values are relative exposure indices, not probabilities and not official warning polygons. They are anchored to the overall AOI hazard level, then adjusted by local terrain. A higher zone value means this sector is more exposed than other monitored sectors if that hazard materializes.

status.notes: elevated risks: storm; Historical context: last 30 days rainfall: 93.1 mm; last 90 days rainfall: 144.6 mm; dry days under 1 mm rain: 15/30; reference evapotranspiration: 71.2 mm/30d; max historical gust: 19.8 m/s; Seasonal baseline from previous 10 years: 30-day rainfall normal 46.2 mm, current ratio 2.01; 90-day rainfall normal 115.0 mm, current ratio 1.26; dry-days normal 22.5, current ratio 0.67.; Elevation context: center is about 52.0 m, sampled range is 52.0-169.0 m, terrain class is rolling. Steeper terrain can increase runoff speed and local flood response; flatter terrain can hold standing water longer.; Highest relative flood-exposure sectors, not probabilities: row 4-column 4 sector, row 7-column 10 sector, row 5-column 10 sector; Prediction summary: 2026-04-25: highest forecast indices are drought 28.6 (low), flood 20.1 (low), storm 13.3 (low).
